title: Legitimisation: GPU Debt, Nuclear Power, and the Asian Buildout
subtitle: CoreWeave's investment-grade GPU loan, nuclear PPAs going mainstream, and Southeast Asia's data centre surge.
summary: Episode Two examines the week of March 28–April 4, 2026, anchored by CoreWeave's landmark $8.5 billion investment-grade debt facility — the first GPU-backed loan to achieve institutional credit ratings. We trace how AI compute infrastructure is being reclassified as an institutional asset class, track NVIDIA's Vera Rubin supply crunch and inference economics, follow the nuclear power-for-data-centres pivot from Meta, Microsoft, and Equinix, and map the accelerating data centre buildout across Southeast Asia and the Middle East. A subsea cable update builds on last week's Red Sea disruption narrative.
takeaways: CoreWeave closed an $8.5B investment-grade debt facility — the first GPU-backed loan rated A3/A (low), backed by $19.2B Meta contracts
Combined hyperscaler 2026 capex approaches $700B: Amazon $200B, Google $175-185B, Meta $115-135B, Microsoft $120B+
NVIDIA Vera Rubin: 3.6 exaFLOPS in NVL72 config; HBM4 memory sold out through 2026, GPU lead times at 36-52 weeks
Inference now accounts for 55% of AI infrastructure spend; costs fell 1,000x in three years to $0.40/million tokens
Meta signed 6.6 GW of nuclear PPAs with Oklo, Vistra, and TerraPower; Equinix is anchoring four separate SMR programmes
Microsoft's Three Mile Island nuclear restart (837 MW) on track for 2028, backed by 20-year PPA and $1.6B Constellation investment
Malaysia now accounts for >50% of under-construction data centre capacity in Southeast Asia; Johor is the prime landing zone
Saudi Arabia's $100B Transcendence AI Initiative and $5.3B AWS commitment signal a Gulf infrastructure buildout of historic scale
Global subsea cable investment running at $13B in 2025-2027; geopolitical rerouting driving a record private cable cycle

chapters.title: Cold Open — The Week Debt Got a GPU Rating
chapters.description: CoreWeave's $8.5B investment-grade loan marks the reclassification of GPU compute infrastructure as an institutional asset class.

chapters.title: CoreWeave's $8.5 Billion Signal
chapters.description: Unpacking the loan structure, the investment-grade rating, Meta concentration risk, and what it means for AI infrastructure financing.

chapters.title: NVIDIA Vera Rubin and the Compute Supply Crunch
chapters.description: Vera Rubin's 3.6 exaFLOPS NVL72 specs, HBM4 sold out through 2026, and why inference cost collapse drives more infrastructure demand.

chapters.title: Nuclear Goes Mainstream: From Pilot to Portfolio
chapters.description: Meta's 6.6 GW nuclear PPAs, Microsoft's Three Mile Island restart, and Equinix's multi-technology SMR portfolio strategy.

chapters.title: Southeast Asia and the Middle East: The Next Buildout Wave
chapters.description: Singapore's DC-CFA2 process, Malaysia's data centre surge, and Saudi Arabia's $100B Transcendence AI Initiative.

chapters.title: Cables: The Infrastructure Beneath the Infrastructure
chapters.description: Building on the Red Sea disruption from Episode 1: $13B in new cable investment, Australia-PNG cables, and Malaysia's SALAM project.

chapters.title: What to Watch — Four Decisions Ahead
chapters.description: CoreWeave credit markets, Singapore DC-CFA2 selections, Vera Rubin supply timeline, and nuclear SMR licensing milestones.

chapters.title: Closing
chapters.description: One word ties the week together: legitimisation — of GPU debt, nuclear power, and Asian infrastructure destinations.

author: Digital Infrastructure Insider
